questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is the Rang Ghar located?
questions.explanation: The Rang Ghar stands near Sivasagar in the state of Assam in northeast India.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar is most often nicknamed which of the following?
questions.explanation: Because it served as a royal amphitheatre, the Rang Ghar is popularly called 'the Colosseum of the East'.

questions.q: What was the Rang Ghar primarily used for?
options: A royal sports and entertainment pavilion
questions.explanation: The Rang Ghar was a two-storey pavilion from which Ahom royalty watched games, fights and other entertainments.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar was built by rulers of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The Rang Ghar was constructed by the Ahom kingdom that ruled much of Assam for about 600 years.

questions.q: How many storeys does the Rang Ghar have?
questions.explanation: The Rang Ghar is a two-storey pavilion, with the royal viewing gallery on the upper floor.

questions.q: Near which historic town in Assam is the Rang Ghar situated?
questions.explanation: The Rang Ghar lies near Sivasagar (formerly Rangpur), once a capital of the Ahom kingdom.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar is regarded as one of Asia's oldest surviving examples of which type of structure?
questions.explanation: It is considered one of the oldest surviving amphitheatres (open-air sports pavilions) in Asia.

questions.q: In which century was the present brick Rang Ghar built?
questions.explanation: The surviving brick structure dates to the 18th century, during the height of Ahom rule.

questions.q: Which large river valley is the Rang Ghar associated with, lying in upper Assam?
questions.explanation: Sivasagar and the Rang Ghar lie in the Brahmaputra valley of upper Assam.

questions.q: What kind of events did Ahom kings most famously watch from the Rang Ghar?
questions.explanation: The royals watched buffalo fights, wrestling, bird fights and other sports, especially during festivals like Rongali Bihu.

questions.q: Which Ahom king is credited with building the present brick Rang Ghar around 1744-1751?
questions.explanation: The present pavilion is generally attributed to Swargadeo Pramatta Singha in the mid-18th century.

questions.q: An earlier version of the Rang Ghar, before the brick one, is said to have been made chiefly of what material?
questions.explanation: An earlier pavilion on the site was built largely of bamboo and timber before the durable brick structure replaced it.

questions.q: The curved roof of the Rang Ghar is said to resemble the inverted form of which traditional object?
questions.explanation: Its distinctive boat-shaped roof is often described as resembling an inverted Ahom royal long boat.

questions.q: The historic mortar used to bind the Rang Ghar's bricks famously included which organic ingredients?
questions.explanation: Ahom builders used a paste of sticky rice and eggs, among other ingredients, instead of modern cement.

questions.q: The name 'Rang Ghar' translates roughly to which of the following?
questions.explanation: In Assamese, 'Rang Ghar' means the 'house of entertainment' or 'house of amusement'.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar is part of the cluster of Ahom monuments around which former capital, also called Rangpur?
questions.explanation: Sivasagar, earlier known as Rangpur, served as an Ahom capital and is dotted with monuments like the Rang Ghar.

questions.q: During which Assamese festival were the games watched from the Rang Ghar especially associated?
questions.explanation: Sporting contests viewed from the Rang Ghar were closely tied to the Rongali (Bohag) Bihu spring festival.

questions.q: How long did the Ahom dynasty, which built the Rang Ghar, rule over much of Assam?
questions.explanation: The Ahom dynasty ruled the region for roughly six centuries, from the early 13th century until the early 19th century.

questions.q: The upper level of the Rang Ghar functioned mainly as what?
questions.explanation: The upper storey served as the gallery from which the king and nobles watched the events below.

questions.q: On top of the Rang Ghar's roof sits a sculpted pair of which animals, a royal emblem?
questions.explanation: The roof is crowned by a sculpted pair of crocodile-like makara figures, an emblem associated with Ahom royalty.

questions.q: Which famous Ahom-built tank (large pond) lies in the same town of Sivasagar as the Rang Ghar?
questions.explanation: The great Sivasagar tank, or Borpukhuri, gives the town its name and lies near the Ahom monuments including the Rang Ghar.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar's design and surviving condition make it a key monument of which broad architectural tradition?
questions.explanation: It is a prime example of the Tai-Ahom architectural tradition of medieval Assam.

questions.q: An earlier timber Rang Ghar is sometimes attributed to which earlier Ahom king, whose reign preceded the brick rebuilding?
questions.explanation: Tradition links an earlier wooden pavilion on the site to the reign of Swargadeo Rudra Singha, later rebuilt in brick by Pramatta Singha.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar's roof, built without a central supporting pillar, is admired chiefly for what engineering feature?
questions.explanation: The boat-shaped roof is a self-supporting curved brick structure, a notable feat of pre-modern Ahom engineering.

questions.q: Which UNESCO World Heritage Site, inscribed in 2024, lies in the same Ahom heartland of upper Assam and reflects the broader Ahom legacy of the Rang Ghar?
questions.explanation: The Charaideo Maidams, the Ahom royal burial mounds, were inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2024 in the Sivasagar region.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar itself is protected and maintained primarily by which national body?
options: Indian National Trust for Art and Cultural Heritage only
questions.explanation: As a centrally protected monument, the Rang Ghar is conserved by the Archaeological Survey of India.

questions.q: Sivasagar, the town near the Rang Ghar, was historically known by which earlier name?
questions.explanation: Sivasagar was earlier called Rangpur and functioned as a capital of the Ahom kingdom.

questions.q: The Ahoms who built the Rang Ghar were originally a people of which broader ethnic origin?
questions.explanation: The Ahoms were a Tai people who migrated into the Brahmaputra valley from Southeast Asia in the early 13th century.

questions.q: The Rang Ghar stands close to the ruins of which adjacent Ahom royal complex used by the kings?
options: The Talatal Ghar / Kareng Ghar at Rangpur
questions.explanation: The Rang Ghar lies near the Talatal Ghar (Kareng Ghar), the Ahom royal palace complex at Rangpur.

questions.q: Why is no central pillar found inside the ground floor of the Rang Ghar?
options: The interior was kept open and the curved roof bears its own load
The pillar collapsed in an earthquake
It was removed by colonial engineers
questions.explanation: The interior was left open so spectators and structure were unobstructed, the curved brick roof being self-supporting without a central column.
